title: FIGURE 3 in HotSHOT Extraction Is a Fast, Economical, and Efficient Method for Obtaining Tardigrade gDNA
author: Amezcua-Martínez, Cuauhtemoc F.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: FIGURE 3: Cytochrome c oxidase I (COI) amplicons from unpurifed gDNA obtained with three diferent gDNA extraction methods. In all cases, upper lane names in the agarose gels are as follows: R, 100-1500 bp DNA ladder; wells 1, 2, 11, and 12 correspond to Mesobiotus sp.; wells 3, 4, 13, and 14 correspond to Hypsibius cf. pallidus; wells 5, 6, 7, 15, 16, and 17 correspond to Minibiotus sidereus; wells 8, 9, 10, 18, 19, and 20 correspond to Minibiotus citlalium; "−", negative control. (a) gDNA obtained using Qiagen kit. (b) gDNA obtained by the chelating resin. (c) gDNA obtained by HotSHOT.
steps_raw: FIGURE 3: Cytochrome c oxidase I (COI) amplicons from unpurifed gDNA obtained with three diferent gDNA extraction methods. In all cases, upper lane names in the agarose gels are as follows: R, 100-1500 bp DNA ladder; wells 1, 2, 11, and 12 correspond to Mesobiotus sp.; wells 3, 4, 13, and 14 correspond to Hypsibius cf. pallidus; wells 5, 6, 7, 15, 16, and 17 correspond to Minibiotus sidereus; wells 8, 9, 10, 18, 19, and 20 correspond to Minibiotus citlalium; "−", negative control. (a) gDNA obtained using Qiagen kit. (b) gDNA obtained by the chelating resin. (c) gDNA obtained by HotSHOT.
